instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same? The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon. It should consist of exactly three paragraphs, with the first paragraph introducing the idea of the book club, the second paragraph outlining its purpose and logistics, and the third paragraph inviting recipients to join. Include a bulleted list in the second paragraph with exactly three points, each containing at least 10 words and starting with the phrase "Key details include." The keyword "book club" must appear at least four times throughout the email, with one instance in the opening sentence and one in the closing sentence. The email must include the phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored."
llm_response:
Hello everyone, I am excited to propose starting a new book club that we can all enjoy together. This book club is designed to be a fun and relaxed way for us to connect over our shared love of reading.

Our main goal is to discuss stories in a friendly environment where everyone feels welcome to share their thoughts. Key details include that we will meet once a month at a local coffee shop to chat about our chosen reads. Key details include that each person should bring one book they have finished before the gathering starts. Key details include that the meeting will last about two hours so we can have plenty of time to talk. We will explore various Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored during our discussions to deepen our understanding of the texts.

Please let me know if you are interested in joining this book club so we can finalize the schedule and pick our first book. I hope you will consider becoming a member of this book club and looking forward to hearing from you soon.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict: it asks for an email that is "easy to read and avoids any jargon," but then explicitly mandates the inclusion of the highly technical, jargon-heavy phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored." The model attempted to follow all instructions, including the jargon requirement, without acknowledging that this requirement contradicts the "no jargon" constraint. It provided a response that follows the structural and content constraints but does not address the logical conflict.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and.new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored."
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and.org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon.
